note: A self-consistent invariant violation: the code activates on an idempotency conflict, and a faithful reader derives the same, so there is NO single-step divergence for bare-next() to catch. The real hazard (a replayed decline returned as a conflict → activate without payment) lives at the external-service boundary and needs a sandbox probe. Scored as CORRECTLY-not-detected — flagging it would be a false alarm; the honest result is silence plus a documented boundary.
